source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Billy Pugh Derrick Basket

event.top_event: Structural failure of Billy Pugh Derrick Basket during personnel lift
actions_taken: Rider grabbed anchor line for support
Rider called emergency descent over radio
Senior Floor Man took control of tugger
Middle Floor Man took control of line spooling guide
Tool Pusher instructed acting Driller to investigate
Emergency descent of basket to rig floor
event.summary: On 14-OCT-2014 at 01:05 hours, during preventive maintenance on the Aft Pipe Racker System, a Short Service Employee was hoisted in a Billy Pugh Derrick Basket to a height of approximately 110 feet. The basket hung up on an unknown part of the derrick/PRS causing it to tilt. Despite radio communication of an 'all stop' command, the basket continued to climb 3-4 inches before stopping. A loud 'pop' was heard and the rider discovered major damage to the single main support arm and two lower arm braces had separated. The rider used the anchor line for support and called for emergency descent. When the basket reached the rig floor, the hanging arm completely separated from the basket.
key_phrases: Failed to develop adequate Written Risk Assessment
Failed to consider high wind conditions (gusting to 30 knots)
Failed to utilize hand signals as required
Radio used as primary communication instead of backup
Flagger failed to hear initial all stop command
Basket hung up on unknown derrick component
Tool Pusher not involved in WRA or supervision
Personnel lacked required man riding training

hazards.name: Personnel lifting with inadequate equipment and controls
High wind conditions during personnel hoisting

threats.name: Basket hangs up on derrick component during ascent
Flagger fails to hear stop command due to high noise area
Basket continues to climb after hang-up causing structural stress
Main support arm and braces fail under load and stress
Inadequate secondary fall protection fails to prevent injury
Anchor line pulls rider to free fall if basket fails

consequences.name: Personnel injury or fatality from basket failure
Structural damage to Billy Pugh Derrick Basket
Damage to derrick and Pipe Racker System
Potential impact to personnel on rig floor from falling basket

controls.name: Written Risk Assessment (WRA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and implement controls

supporting_text: Failed to develop an adequate Written Risk Assessment prior to performing the task to assess all threats of injury to personnel, damage to equipment or harm to the environment
Failed to consider the weather (high winds-gusting up to 30 knots) as an environmental factor of safety in the WRA prior to performing the job task

controls.name: Hand signals as primary communication method
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure clear communication during lifting operations

supporting_text: Failed to utilize hand signals as stated in the WRA. The use of radios was to be used as backup only

WRA-DID-1001 Work Basket Operations-Rig Floor step number three states in red 'Note: Radios are not to be used. Hand signals are the only means to be used for man riding operations. Radios may be used as back up only'
According to witness statements it was agreed radios would be the primary means of communication instead due to the basket being hoisted to a height of 110 feet above the rig floor

controls.name: Flagger monitoring and visual contact
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to maintain visual oversight of lifting operation
controls.lod_basis: WRA requirement for tugger operator to maintain visual contact

supporting_text: Lessee failed to ensure the hazard controls were in place as written in the WRA. According to the WRA, the Tugger Operator shall maintain visual contact at all times. The designated flagger was assigned additional task of monitoring the radio while operating the line guide spooler

The flagger failed to hear the initial 'all stop' on the hand held radios and the Tugger Operator continued to raise the basket causing the occurrence of major damage to the single support arm and separation of the lower arm braces

controls.name: Secondary fall protection with self-retracting lifeline
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent personnel injury from fall
controls.lod_basis: OEM recommended practice for DB-1 basket operations

supporting_text: Failed to utilize proper secondary fall protection which created a threat of injury to personnel both in the basket and on the rig floor. The rider attached his full body harness lanyard to the inside of the basket handle (instead of above the air hoist line or another anchor point outside of the basket with a self-retracting lifeline (SRL) as per the Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM) Recommended Practice)
All personnel riding on a DB-1 should wear an approved full body harness attached to a certified retractable lifeline designed for this purpose

controls.name: OEM nylon safety strap installation
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to secure basket and prevent structural failure
controls.lod_basis: OEM recommended practice for DB-1 basket operations

supporting_text: Failed to ensure the OEM nylon safety strap was present and being utilized as recommended by OEM by attaching the top loop of the nylon strap along with the pad eye of the derrick basket utilizing a shackle from the tugger line to the derrick basket prior to man riding operations

controls.name: Tool Pusher supervision and involvement in WRA
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper oversight and planning

supporting_text: Failed to ensure the on tour Tool Pusher was involved in Written Risk Assessment prior to and during the man riding operation as required in the contractor policy, both WRA's, and the man riding checklist
Failed to ensure the on tour Tool Pusher supervised the man riding operation as required in the contractor policy, both WRS's, and the man riding checklist

After hearing the 'emergency, lower down easy' radio transmission from the rider in the basket, the on tour Tool Pusher failed to supervise the emergency decent of the rider and basket and continued to prepare paperwork from the Driller's cabin
The on tour Tool Pusher was inside the Driller's cabin attending to paperwork

controls.name: Weather assessment and Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent operations in unsafe conditions
controls.lod_basis: OEM recommended practice and safety management

supporting_text: Failed to consider the weather (high winds-gusting up to 30 knots) as an environmental factor of safety in the WRA prior to performing the job task
Failed to initiate SWA due to the poor weather conditions at the time of the job
DB-1 should not be utilized in weather, wind, or conditions the qualified person or rider considers to be unsafe

controls.name: Personnel training and competency verification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel are qualified for task

supporting_text: Failed to recognize the rider inside the basket and Middle Floor Man had not completed the contract driller's required man riding training before becoming involved with man riding operations, but instead it was recommended to be a good experience for learning

controls.name: Rescue Plan development and communication
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to enable emergency response

supporting_text: It was discovered a Rescue Plan was written, but witness statements verify prior to the man riding operation, the drill crew failed to discuss the Rescue Plan. There was no Rescue Plan detailing retrieval procedures for a rider in the BPDB without proper secondary personal fall protection

controls.name: OEM equipment knowledge and compliance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper equipment operation

supporting_text: Was not aware of the Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM) Recommended Practice for using the basket or the 400 pound safe working load rating of the basket (4,000 pound tensile strength)
Failed to know or follow the Original Equipment Manufacturers requirements for operating the BPDB

controls.name: Appropriate lifting equipment selection
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to match equipment capacity to load
controls.lod_basis: Equipment specifications and safe working load

supporting_text: Failed to recognize the threat of personnel injury, or damage to equipment by lifting personnel with a utility winch with a hoisting capacity which far exceeded the tensile strength of the basket
The crew also utilized the utility tugger (14,000 pounds hoisting capacity) to hoist the basket

controls.name: Fingerboard monitor positioning
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to maintain visual oversight at height
controls.lod_basis: Operational best practice for personnel hoisting

supporting_text: Failed to station a team member on the fingerboard to monitor activity, maintain close proximity, or communication since radio communication was being utilized and the Tugger Operator could not maintain visual contact

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
